title: Phot.Venice.Gondolas.Rialto.01.041025.5052.jpg
tags: italy venice nikon d800 d300 d200 e5700 f2 f3 f4 coolpix frankartculinaryyahoode italien italia venedig venezia venise harry’sbar bellini murano burano square places place plaza plätze strasen rue calle strada streets market gondolas gondoliere yacht sailing piazza sanmarco gondeln gondole góndola voga canalgrande rialto cruiseliner “squero san trovaso” “san giorgio maggiore” “piazzetta di marco”
description: Italy, Venice, near Ponte Rialto, the lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. If the buildings would be straight, painted & well maintained, I think Venice without his patina, the gondolas, channels, little bridges & the at all-time busy Canal Grande; these main ingredients altogether express the romantic charm of Venice.    There are officially 425 Gondolas, 2010, in Venice with a license, which is hard to get & stays normally in the family. A license for a Gondoliere can cost up to 500.000 €, on the other hand, a Gondoliere makes between 10 & 15 thousand € a month, …stand 2010  Modern gondolas are up to 11 mtr long, 1.6 mtr wide & a weight of about 350 kg, made of 280 hand-made pieces using eight types of wood. It takes about 200 hours to manufacture a gondola; at a cost of roughly 30,000 €.  To compensate for unilateral propulsion, the gondola body is built asymmetrically along the central axis; the left side is curved more than the right starboard side, so that its contour on the starboard side is about 0.25 m shorter than on the port side.  The several mtr long oar-paddle is held in an o complicated shaped fork, known as a Fórcola, which is inserted into a rectangular opening in the hull on the starboard side.  The Forcola is allowing several positions of the oar for slow forward rowing, powerful forward rowing, turning, slowing down, rowing backwards stopping, shaped individually for each gondoliers style.  The ornament iron, called the "fero da prorà" or "dolfin", on the front of the boat is made from brass, stainless steel, or aluminium. It serves as decoration; every detail of the gondola has its own symbolism & needed to balance the weight of the gondolier at the stern, has an "S" shape symbolic of the twists in the Canal Grande. Under the main blade, there is a kind of comb with six teeth or prongs pointing forward standing for the six districts of Venice. A kind of tooth juts out backwards toward the centre of the gondola symbolises the island of Giudecca, the curved top signifies the Doge's cap.  Originally, the designs were not uniform & changed over the centuries. Originally, there were gondolas in all possible colours. The Venetian noble & patrician houses at the time tried to outdo each other in the magnificent equipment of the boats. In order to put an end to this unbridled pomposity, the Senate of Venice in 1562 passed the expense law. Supported by the church, this law prescribed uniform black equipment for all gondolas; there were exceptions for foreign envoys & for festivals.  👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  16  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

The construction of the "Vajoliroja" had a cost of around € 29 million.   “Vajoliroja”, the 47,55 mtr long, in 1920s neo-classic style charter yacht was built 2001in Turkey by Turquoise Yacht Construction in Turkey. After been refitted in 2008/9 & later renamed “Amphitrite”. Bestowing cabins for a maximum of 10 guests staying on board, the Vajoliroja accommodates everyone comfortably. This ship has 7 to 9 proficient crew members to maintain & sail, taking care of the guests in 5 staterooms, including the elegant & spacious master suite, located forward on the main deck, with an adjoining study area & bathroom in suite, 1VIP stateroom, 1 double cabin, four cabins are situated on the lower deck comprising of three double / twin cabins, an upper & lower bedded cabin with additional Pullman berth.  A 7 mtr custom built Riva-style tender stowed forward on the main deck ensuring uncluttered after deck areas for guests to enjoy the al fresco dining in optimum privacy & comfort. title: Phot.Venice.Harry's.Bar.Bellini.01.041025.5271.jpg
tags: italy venice nikon d800 d300 d200 e5700 f2 f3 f4 coolpix frankartculinaryyahoode italien italia venedig venezia venise harry’sbar bellini murano burano square places place plaza plätze strasen rue calle strada streets market gondolas gondoliere yacht sailing piazza sanmarco gondeln gondole góndola voga canalgrande rialto cruiseliner “squero san trovaso
description: Italy, Venice, the first & original "Harry’s Bar", opened by Giuseppe Cipriani in Venice in March 1931, named after ”Harry Pickering” who gave Cipriani, a Bartender at the Hotel Europe, the first money to open his own bar, today there are numerous franchises worldwide,   Guiseppe Cipriano is also the creator, around in the forties, of the classic long drink “Bellini”, marrow from sun-ripened pink peaches & Prosecco Spumante the drink is simple but great & at 22€, 2010, a cocktail highly overpriced like all other cocktails at Harris Bar too.  By the way, the “Beef Carpaccio”, created at the five star hotel Cipriani & owner of the “Harry’s Bar”, still served in the original way, not thinly sliced on a meat slicing machine, rather the raw beef tenderloin is flattened between foil, seasoned with a creamy kind of mayonnaise dressing & served with a small side salad at over 60 € is also absolutely highly overpriced, even so it was good, ... in general food & beverage is tasty & well prepared,…  however, prices are in no relation to quality & value,…  well,... "Hemmingway's Ghost" makes  it possible.  📌... If it would not have been for Hemmingway, who choose this place as his favoured bar in Venice as his hangout & consequently also by many other famous customers from culture, art, show business/Hollywood & business “joint the club”. Just to mention a few like Charlie Chaplin, Princess Aspasia of Greece, Alfred Hitchcock, Jimmy Stewart, Truman Capote, Arturo Toscanini, Orson Welles, Baron Philippe de Rothschild, Aristotle Onassis, Barbara Hutton, Peggy Guggenheim, Woody Allen, George Cloone & today mainly by simple tourists like me.  My main reason to visit the Harry’s Bar was simply because of my curiosity for food & beverage & gastronomical historically well-known locations. Without its past heritage it would be just another simple corner bar without any special flair & a service attitude like, "just another tourist-voyeur we will not see again", seems they don’t care about a good tip for adequate service.  Anyway, ... I was there, I saw & felt the "atmosphere",  it was not good, it was not bad,....it was just the "opposite", ⦂⚪)… cheers…🍸,  ... like "Alfredos" restaurant in Rome, the creator of "Fettuccine Alfredo", I came, I saw & I'm enriched now for one more "one-time experience" & that's enough, "Veni, Vidi, Effugit",  👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  16  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: Italy, Venice, "Marala", a classic 1930s luxury motor yacht, integrated with the latest in modern entertainment, safety & navigation systems. The accommodation capacity offering seven comfortable cabins for a maximum of 13 yacht guests spending the nights & under normal conditions she has room for approx. 18 expert crew members to operate the yacht. The steel hull, aluminium & steel superstructure yacht is 58.8 mtr long, a beam of 8.08 mtr, a deep draught of almost 4 mtr, two 750 Horse Power main engines, reaching a high speed of 15 knots  = 27.78 km built with a teak wood deck by Camper & Nicholsons ship building company in Southampton, United Kingdom  📌 ....if you still have some 10 million++€ lying around somewhere & don't need it, you can buy the Marala with it, …just for nice weekends, photo shootings or so. title: VENEZIA. RIO DEL MALCANTON
tags: venezia venice venise venecia venedig ve veneto taxi motoscafo barca canale rio acqua riodelmalcanton dorsoduro malcanton palazzo calle building unesco sitounesco patrimoniodellumanità italia italy italie italien bellitalia turismo centrocittà centrostorico cittàdarte serenissima canon eos6dmarkii 6dmarkii canoneos6dmarkii canon6dmarkii franco600d 1001nightsthenew 1001nightsmagicwindow 1001nightsmagichall
description: IL TAXI N. 166   Tra le calli di Dorsoduro verità e mito si intrecciano perdendosi l’uno nell’altra: alcuni luoghi emanano un’aura di mistero e raccontano storie che solo i veneziani conoscono. Puoi scegliere di camminare sul Rio del Malcanton, luogo, si dice, un tempo prediletto dai ladri per derubare gli ignari passanti (da questo deriva il nome “Malcanton”, cioè “angolo malfamato”). O ancora, puoi salire sul Ponte dei Pugni, sulla cui sommità si battevano due fazioni di Venezia, i Nicolotti contro i Castellani. Sulla sommità del ponte ancora oggi sono tracciate le impronte del punto dove i contendenti si posizionavano prima di darsele di santa ragione. Puoi infine passare attraverso Sotoportego del Casin dei Nobili, la casa frequentata solo da nobili Veneziani, che di nobile aveva ben poco. Al suo interno si dice infatti ci si dedicasse a gioco d’azzardo e ci si intrattenesse in gentil compagnia femminile.   CANON EOS 6D Mark II con ob. CANON EF 24-85 f./3,5-4,5 USM

title: VENEZIA. PASSEGGIATA IN RELAX
tags: venezia ve venice venise venecia venedig veneto calle vicolo street strada coppia giovani ragazzi passeggio turisti tourist bw bianconero monocromo blackandwhite blackwhite canon eos6dmarkii 6dmarkii canoneos6dmarkii canon6dmarkii franco600d 1584 35 10 flickrblackandwhite
description: QUANT'E' BELLA GIOVINEZZA.   La costruzione di Venezia, apparentemente galleggiante sulle acque dell’Adriatico, fu la più grande sfida che gli abitanti dovettero affrontare , creando fondamenta stabili in grado di sorreggere il peso degli edifici. L’ingegnosità con cui la città è stata costruita è notevole, quasi più del suo fascino. Venezia poggia su un centinaio di isolotti fangosi, in mezzo alla laguna paludosa, che per far fronte alle condizioni cosi proibitive, i primi costruttori veneziani svilupparono tecniche del tutto originali, utilizzando pietra impermeabile sostenute da zattere di larici e pali di pino provenienti dalla Alpi. I quali non marciscono nel suolo, perché manca l’ossigeno necessario ai microbi. Il tessuto di Venezia è costituito da una serie di comunità insulari autonome collegate fra loro da ponti. I quali erano spesso privati e si doveva pagare un pedaggio per usarli, che in origine non avevano ringhiere o balaustre, cosi creando molti pericoli a chi li usava di notte. Ciascun campo ha la sua riserva d’acqua, il pozzo, unica fonte di acqua fresca, che attraverso griglie nei pavimenti, raccoglieva l’acqua piovana in cisterne foderate d’argilla e riempita di sabbia che agiva come filtro.   CANON EOS 6D Mark II con ob. CANON EF 24-85 f./3,5-4,5 USM

title: Phot.Venice.Harry's.Bar.Bellini.01.041025.5271.jpg
tags: italy venice nikon d800 d300 d200 e5700 f2 f3 f4 coolpix frankartculinaryyahoode italien italia venedig venezia venise harry’sbar bellini murano burano square places place plaza plätze strasen rue calle strada streets market gondolas gondoliere yacht sailing piazza sanmarco gondeln gondole góndola voga canalgrande rialto cruiseliner “squero san trovaso
description: Italy, Venice, the first & original "Harry’s Bar", opened by Giuseppe Cipriani in Venice in March 1931, named after ”Harry Pickering” who gave Cipriani, a Bartender at the Hotel Europe, the first money to open his own bar, today there are numerous franchises worldwide,   Guiseppe Cipriano is also the creator, around in the forties, of the classic long drink “Bellini”, marrow from sun-ripened pink peaches & Prosecco Spumante the drink is simple but great & at 22€, 2010, a cocktail highly overpriced like all other cocktails at Harris Bar too.  By the way, the “Beef Carpaccio”, created at the five star hotel Cipriani & owner of the “Harry’s Bar”, still served in the original way, not thinly sliced on a meat slicing machine, rather the raw beef tenderloin is flattened between foil, seasoned with a creamy kind of mayonnaise dressing & served with a small side salad at over 60 € is also absolutely highly overpriced, even so it was good, ... in general food & beverage is tasty & well prepared,…  however, prices are in no relation to quality & value,…  well,... "Hemmingway's Ghost" makes  it possible.  📌... If it would not have been for Hemmingway, who choose this place as his favoured bar in Venice as his hangout & consequently also by many other famous customers from culture, art, show business/Hollywood & business “joint the club”. Just to mention a few like Charlie Chaplin, Princess Aspasia of Greece, Alfred Hitchcock, Jimmy Stewart, Truman Capote, Arturo Toscanini, Orson Welles, Baron Philippe de Rothschild, Aristotle Onassis, Barbara Hutton, Peggy Guggenheim, Woody Allen, George Cloone & today mainly by simple tourists like me.  My main reason to visit the Harry’s Bar was simply because of my curiosity for food & beverage & gastronomical historically well-known locations. Without its past heritage it would be just another simple corner bar without any special flair & a service attitude like, "just another tourist-voyeur we will not see again", seems they don’t care about a good tip for adequate service.  Anyway, ... I was there, I saw & felt the "atmosphere",  it was not good, it was not bad,....it was just the "opposite", ⦂⚪)… cheers…🍸,  ... like "Alfredos" restaurant in Rome, the creator of "Fettuccine Alfredo", I came, I saw & I'm enriched now for one more "one-time experience" & that's enough, "Veni, Vidi, Effugit",  👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  16  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

title: VENEZIA. RIO DEL MALCANTON
tags: venezia venice venise venecia venedig ve veneto taxi motoscafo barca canale rio acqua riodelmalcanton dorsoduro malcanton palazzo calle building unesco sitounesco patrimoniodellumanità italia italy italie italien bellitalia turismo centrocittà centrostorico cittàdarte serenissima canon eos6dmarkii 6dmarkii canoneos6dmarkii canon6dmarkii franco600d 1001nightsthenew 1001nightsmagicwindow 1001nightsmagichall
description: IL TAXI N. 166   Tra le calli di Dorsoduro verità e mito si intrecciano perdendosi l’uno nell’altra: alcuni luoghi emanano un’aura di mistero e raccontano storie che solo i veneziani conoscono. Puoi scegliere di camminare sul Rio del Malcanton, luogo, si dice, un tempo prediletto dai ladri per derubare gli ignari passanti (da questo deriva il nome “Malcanton”, cioè “angolo malfamato”). O ancora, puoi salire sul Ponte dei Pugni, sulla cui sommità si battevano due fazioni di Venezia, i Nicolotti contro i Castellani. Sulla sommità del ponte ancora oggi sono tracciate le impronte del punto dove i contendenti si posizionavano prima di darsele di santa ragione. Puoi infine passare attraverso Sotoportego del Casin dei Nobili, la casa frequentata solo da nobili Veneziani, che di nobile aveva ben poco. Al suo interno si dice infatti ci si dedicasse a gioco d’azzardo e ci si intrattenesse in gentil compagnia femminile.   CANON EOS 6D Mark II con ob. CANON EF 24-85 f./3,5-4,5 USM

description: Italy, Venice, …does it always has to be a gondola in Venice? How about cruising through Venice's canals in a water-stretch-limousine.  The lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. If the buildings would be straight, painted & well maintained, I think Venice without this patina, the gondolas & gondolieri, channels, little bridges & the at all-time busy Canal Grande; all these main ingredients altogether express the romantic charm of Venice.    👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  16 million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comment

title: Phot.Venice.Railway.Santa Lucia.01.041025.5096.jpg
tags: italy venice nikon d800 d300 d200 f2 f3 f4 coolpix frankartculinaryyahoode italien italia venedig venezia venise murano burano square places place plaza plätze strasen rue calle strada streets historic market gondolas gondoliere yacht sailing piazza sanmarco gondeln gondole góndola voga canalgrande rialto cruiseliner
description: Italy, Venice, Stazione di Venezia Santa Lucia. Trains carry customers across the lagoon to Venice's Santa Lucia train station, right on the Grand Canal. The station, built in the 1940s & 1950s, required the demolition of the Church of Santa Lucia, which is where the station gets its name. Venice also as a day-trip destination handles roughly 82,000 people using the station every day with connections to cities throughout Italy &  major cities in Europe.  The station opens onto the Grand Canal in Venice's Cannaregio district. The Ponte dei Scalzi bridge is close to the station's entrance & crosses the Grand Canal to the Santa Croce district. There is a vaporetto, the water bus stop right in front of the station, the Ponte della Costituzione connects the station with the Piazzale Roma parking lot & cruise terminal.  Since the 1860s there's been a station here, named after the church of Santa Lucia which was demolished to make way for it. Until 1933 Venice's islands could only be accessed by river boats or railway. The design of the current station dates from a competition held in 1934, but construction took considerable time & the station was only completed in 1952, since then, a terminal has been built for road transport with car parks & bus stations.  The design from 1934 of the current station was won by Virgilio Vallot in a contest. Between 1936 & 1943, Mazzoni & Vallot collaborated on the construction of the station building. The final implementation was undertaken only after the Second World War.  In 1952, the station was finalized on a design by the architect Paul Perilli.   👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  13  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: Italy, Venice, "Giardini della Biennale", an area of parkland in the historic city of Venice which hosts a major part of the city's cultural Biennale Art Festival.  A light breeze of spicy sea air from the Adriatic, the whisper of the leaves in the trees & bushes, no sound of the vaporetti, water taxis & barges. This park not far from the city centre, on the eastern edge of Venice is a green oasis of relaxation not only for the Venetians. An exception is, when like every two years, the cultural event "La Biennale Art Exhibitions" takes place between May & November.  The gardens were created by Napoleon Bonaparte who drained an area of marshland in order to create a public garden on the banks of the Bacino di San Marco. The traditional site of La Biennale Art Exhibitions since the first edition in 1895, the Giardini were made by Napoleon at the beginning of the nineteenth century. It was the success of the first editions to trigger the building of foreign pavilions since 1907, which were added to the already built Central Pavilion. The Giardini now host 29 pavilions of foreign countries, some of them designed by famous architects.  The lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. If the buildings would be straight, painted & well maintained, I think Venice without this patina, the gondolas & gondolieri, channels, little bridges & the at all-time busy Canal Grande; all these main ingredients altogether express the romantic charm of Venice.    👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  13  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

title: Phot.Venice.Market.Fish.01.041027.5769.jpg
tags: italy venice nikon d800 d300 d200 f2 f3 f4 coolpix frankartculinaryyahoode italien italia venedig venezia venise murano burano square places place plaza plätze strasen rue calle strada streets historic market gondolas gondoliere yacht sailing piazza sanmarco gondeln gondole góndola voga canalgrande rialto cruiseliner
description: Italy, Venice, "Mercato del Pesce al Minuto" near Ponte Rialto. Seafood in great variety & of the highest quality of the particular menagerie of scaled, tentacled & hard-shelled sea creatures are on display at the market on five mornings a week.  Freshest & finest quality is an obsession by the only less than 70,000 inhabitants of Venice. The market is not huge, about thirty fishmongers & their assistants stand behind tables glistening with fish guts & brine beneath a canvas canopy supported by colonnades in the Venetian neo-Gothic style. The present construction dates only from the early 20th century, but seafood has been sold at the Rialto for at least 500 years. An inscription in ornamental metalwork over a nearby gate reads "Piscis premium a capite Foetet"- Fish starts rotting from the head", this old saying once had political as well as piscatorial character.  The assortment of seafood differs from season to season & even can varies from day to day. Unless the fish is identified as frozen, there is a bit of this, obviously dried or salted, you may be sure that everything sold here is as fresh as it can be.  Not everything in the market is fished in local waters, the fishing grounds of the northern Adriatic are closed annually for 40 days between July & September to allow stocks to regenerate, during that period everything comes from somewhere else, except for the fish, including the famous & delicate branzini, or sea bass, that are farmed in the area, but what does come from the lagoon usually sells for a premium a local seafood & is labelled "Nostrano", "ours," a standard term all over Italy.  The choice & freshness of the fish & seafood displayed on ice is hard to beat; comparable in central Europe I have only found in France & the Mediterranean region, even so we have a good fish market in Hamburg, but cannot be compared to this choice.  The lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. If the buildings would be straight, painted & well maintained, I think Venice without this patina, the gondolas & gondolieri, channels, little bridges & the at all-time busy Canal Grande; all these main ingredients altogether express the romantic charm of Venice.    👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  13  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

title: Phot.Venice.Market.Fish.01.041027.5817.jpg
tags: italy venice nikon d800 d300 d200 f2 f3 f4 coolpix frankartculinaryyahoode italien italia venedig venezia venise murano burano square places place plaza plätze strasen rue calle strada streets historic market gondolas gondoliere yacht sailing piazza sanmarco gondeln gondole góndola voga canalgrande rialto cruiseliner
description: Italy, Venice, "Mercato del Pesce al Minuto", the market near Ponte Rialto displays Seafood in great variety & of the highest quality of the particular menagerie of scaled, tentacled & hard-shelled sea creatures are on display at the market on five mornings a week.  Freshest & finest quality is an obsession by the only less than 70,000 inhabitants of Venice. The market is not huge, about thirty fishmongers & their assistants stand behind tables glistening with fish guts & brine beneath a canvas canopy supported by colonnades in the Venetian neo-Gothic style. The present construction dates only from the early 20th century, but seafood has been sold at the Rialto for at least 500 years. An inscription in ornamental metalwork over a nearby gate reads "Piscis premium a capite Foetet"- Fish starts rotting from the head", this old saying once had political as well as piscatorial character.  The assortment of seafood differs from season to season & even can varies from day to day. Unless the fish is identified as frozen, there is a bit of this, obviously dried or salted, you may be sure that everything sold here is as fresh as it can be.  Not everything in the market is fished in local waters, the fishing grounds of the northern Adriatic are closed annually for 40 days between July & September to allow stocks to regenerate, during that period everything comes from somewhere else, except for the fish, including the famous & delicate branzini, or sea bass, that are farmed in the area, but what does come from the lagoon usually sells for a premium a local seafood & is labelled "Nostrano", "ours," a standard term all over Italy.  The choice & freshness of the fish & seafood displayed on ice is hard to beat; comparable in central Europe I have only found in France & the Mediterranean region, even so we have a good fish market in Hamburg, but cannot be compared to this choice.  The lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. If the buildings would be straight, painted & well maintained, I think Venice without this patina, the gondolas & gondolieri, channels, little bridges & the at all-time busy Canal Grande; all these main ingredients altogether express the romantic charm of Venice.    👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  13  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

title: Phot.Venice.Railway.Santa Lucia.01.041025.5096.jpg
tags: italy venice nikon d800 d300 d200 f2 f3 f4 coolpix frankartculinaryyahoode italien italia venedig venezia venise murano burano square places place plaza plätze strasen rue calle strada streets historic market gondolas gondoliere yacht sailing piazza sanmarco gondeln gondole góndola voga canalgrande rialto cruiseliner
description: Italy, Venice, Stazione di Venezia Santa Lucia. Trains carry customers across the lagoon to Venice's Santa Lucia train station, right on the Grand Canal. The station, built in the 1940s & 1950s, required the demolition of the Church of Santa Lucia, which is where the station gets its name. Venice also as a day-trip destination handles roughly 82,000 people using the station every day with connections to cities throughout Italy &  major cities in Europe.  The station opens onto the Grand Canal in Venice's Cannaregio district. The Ponte dei Scalzi bridge is close to the station's entrance & crosses the Grand Canal to the Santa Croce district. There is a vaporetto, the water bus stop right in front of the station, the Ponte della Costituzione connects the station with the Piazzale Roma parking lot & cruise terminal.  Since the 1860s there's been a station here, named after the church of Santa Lucia which was demolished to make way for it. Until 1933 Venice's islands could only be accessed by river boats or railway. The design of the current station dates from a competition held in 1934, but construction took considerable time & the station was only completed in 1952, since then, a terminal has been built for road transport with car parks & bus stations.  The design from 1934 of the current station was won by Virgilio Vallot in a contest. Between 1936 & 1943, Mazzoni & Vallot collaborated on the construction of the station building. The final implementation was undertaken only after the Second World War.  In 1952, the station was finalized on a design by the architect Paul Perilli.   👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  13  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: Italy, Venice, Church of Santa Maria Zobenigo, also known as Chiesa di Santa Maria del Giglio. A baroque Roman Catholic Church built by Giuseppe Sardi for Admiral Antonio Barbaro between 1678 & 1681. The church has one of the finest Venetian Baroque facades in all of Venice.   The facade lacks any Christian image statues or reliefs. It shows the marble relief maps of various places in which Antonio Barbaro served, also including his own statue, in the centre, other statues are his brothers.  Along the nave are painted depictions of the Via Crucis or Stations of the Cross by various artists, the nave ceiling is decorated with a large canvas by Antonio Zanchi. The Molin chapel contains a Madonna & Child, the only painting by the Flemish painter Rubens in Venice. The altar has flanking statues, behind the high altar, in the sanctuary are paintings of several famous Italian painters.  The lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. If the buildings would be straight, painted & well maintained, I think Venice without this patina, the gondolas & gondolieri, channels, little bridges & the at all-time busy Canal Grande; all these main ingredients altogether express the romantic charm of Venice.    👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  14  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: Italy, Venice, view over the top of the “Torre dell’Orologio”, the clock tower at the Piazza di San Marco. The two great bronze figures strike each full hours on the bell. One statue represents an old person, the other one a young person, to symbolise the transitory of time. They sculptures are acknowledged as "the Moors" because of the dark patina acquired by the bronze. The bell is also original & is signed by one Simeone who cast it at the Arsenal in 1497.  The Tower & its large Astronomical Clock, a masterpiece of technology & engineering, form an essential part of the Venice image & the archway has been for over 500 years one of the most famous architectural landmarks in Venice, erect over an arch that leads into the main shopping street of the city, the old Merceria & to the Rialto.  The lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. description: Italy, Venice, the entrance to the “Arsenale” at Campo de Arsenale with the wooden bridge crossing the Rio dell' Arsenale The Arsenale used to be originally Venice’s shipyard, founded in 1104. Nowadays it belongs to the Italian marine corps & not accessible to the public, the only exception is every two years during the “Biennale”, the oldest established forum for contemporary art.  The arsenal was surrounded by walls & towers like a fortress, to which there were only two entrances; the water gate, "Ingresso all'Acqua", which is flanked by two towers erected in 1574 & if necessary, could be closed off by a gate. Through this gate, shipping traffic was handled over the harbour basin into the Mediterranean.  The land gate, portal gate - "Ingresso di Terra". The Land Gate is located next to the Water Gate, which in its current form was built around 1460 in the manner of a triumphal arch to show that Venice is famous for its wealth & fortune. The entrance stairs are flanked by 8 symbolic figures. The two giant ancient lions to the right & left of the stairs are loot pieces that Francesco Morosini brought from Greece to Venice in 1687. The left three-meter-high lion had been near Athens in Porto Leone for two thousand years.  The lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. If the buildings would be straight, painted & well maintained, I think Venice without this patina, the gondolas & gondolieri, channels, little bridges & the at all-time busy Canal Grande; all these main ingredients altogether express the romantic charm of Venice.    👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  14  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

tags: italy venice nikon d800 d300 d200 f2 f3 f4 coolpix frankartculinaryyahoode italien italia venedig venezia venise murano burano square places place plaza plätze strasen rue calle strada streets historic market gondolas gondoliere yacht sailing piazza sanmarco gondeln gondole góndola voga canalgrande rialto cruiseliner
description: Italy, Venice, Church of Santa Maria Zobenigo, also known as Chiesa di Santa Maria del Giglio. A baroque Roman Catholic Church built by Giuseppe Sardi for Admiral Antonio Barbaro between 1678 & 1681. The church has one of the finest Venetian Baroque facades in all of Venice.   The facade lacks any Christian image statues or reliefs. It shows the marble relief maps of various places in which Antonio Barbaro served, also including his own statue, in the centre, other statues are his brothers.  Along the nave are painted depictions of the Via Crucis or Stations of the Cross by various artists, the nave ceiling is decorated with a large canvas by Antonio Zanchi. The Molin chapel contains a Madonna & Child, the only painting by the Flemish painter Rubens in Venice. The altar has flanking statues, behind the high altar, in the sanctuary are paintings of several famous Italian painters.  The lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. If the buildings would be straight, painted & well maintained, I think Venice without this patina, the gondolas & gondolieri, channels, little bridges & the at all-time busy Canal Grande; all these main ingredients altogether express the romantic charm of Venice.    👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  14  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

title: Phot.Venice.Market.Fish.01.041027.5821.jpg
tags: italy venice nikon d800 d300 d200 f2 f3 f4 coolpix frankartculinaryyahoode italien italia venedig venezia venise murano burano square places place plaza plätze strasen rue calle strada streets historic market gondolas gondoliere yacht sailing piazza sanmarco gondeln gondole góndola voga canalgrande rialto cruiseliner
description: Italy, Venice, "Mercato del Pesce al Minuto", In front the delicate John Dory, better known as St. Pierre / St. Pietro along many other names like the many related legends about the names of this fish. The most spread legend says that the dark spot on the fish's flank is St. Peter's thumbprint.  The market near Ponte Rialto displays Seafood in great variety & of the highest quality of the particular menagerie of scaled, tentacled & hard-shelled sea creatures are on display at the market on five mornings a week.  Freshest & finest quality is an obsession by the only less than 70,000 inhabitants of Venice. The market is not huge, about thirty fishmongers & their assistants stand behind tables glistening with fish guts & brine beneath a canvas canopy supported by colonnades in the Venetian neo-Gothic style. The present construction dates only from the early 20th century, but seafood has been sold at the Rialto for at least 500 years. An inscription in ornamental metalwork over a nearby gate reads "Piscis premium a capite Foetet"- Fish starts rotting from the head", this old saying once had political as well as piscatorial character.  The assortment of seafood differs from season to season & even can varies from day to day. Unless the fish is identified as frozen, there is a bit of this, obviously dried or salted, you may be sure that everything sold here is as fresh as it can be.  Not everything in the market is fished in local waters, the fishing grounds of the northern Adriatic are closed annually for 40 days between July & September to allow stocks to regenerate, during that period everything comes from somewhere else, except for the fish, including the famous & delicate branzini, or sea bass, that are farmed in the area, but what does come from the lagoon usually sells for a premium a local seafood & is labelled "Nostrano", "ours," a standard term all over Italy.  The choice & freshness of the fish & seafood displayed on ice is hard to beat; comparable in central Europe I have only found in France & the Mediterranean region, even so we have a good fish market in Hamburg, but cannot be compared to this choice.  The lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. If the buildings would be straight, painted & well maintained, I think Venice without this patina, the gondolas & gondolieri, channels, little bridges & the at all-time busy Canal Grande; all these main ingredients altogether express the romantic charm of Venice.   👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  13  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: Italy, Venice, "Mercato del Pesce al Minuto", the market near Ponte Rialto displays Seafood in great variety & of the highest quality of the particular menagerie of scaled, tentacled & hard-shelled sea creatures are on display at the market on five mornings a week.  Freshest & finest quality is an obsession by the only less than 70,000 inhabitants of Venice. The market is not huge, about thirty fishmongers & their assistants stand behind tables glistening with fish guts & brine beneath a canvas canopy supported by colonnades in the Venetian neo-Gothic style. The present construction dates only from the early 20th century, but seafood has been sold at the Rialto for at least 500 years. An inscription in ornamental metalwork over a nearby gate reads "Piscis premium a capite Foetet"- Fish starts rotting from the head", this old saying once had political as well as piscatorial character.  The assortment of seafood differs from season to season & even can varies from day to day. Unless the fish is identified as frozen, there is a bit of this, obviously dried or salted, you may be sure that everything sold here is as fresh as it can be.  Not everything in the market is fished in local waters, the fishing grounds of the northern Adriatic are closed annually for 40 days between July & September to allow stocks to regenerate, during that period everything comes from somewhere else, except for the fish, including the famous & delicate branzini, or sea bass, that are farmed in the area, but what does come from the lagoon usually sells for a premium a local seafood & is labelled "Nostrano", "ours," a standard term all over Italy.  The choice & freshness of the fish & seafood displayed on ice is hard to beat; comparable in central Europe I have only found in France & the Mediterranean region, even so we have a good fish market in Hamburg, but cannot be compared to this choice.  The lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. If the buildings would be straight, painted & well maintained, I think Venice without this patina, the gondolas & gondolieri, channels, little bridges & the at all-time busy Canal Grande; all these main ingredients altogether express the romantic charm of Venice.    👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  13  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

title: Phot.Venice.Gondoliere.Bridge.01.041023.4620.jpg
tags: italy venice nikon d800 d300 d200 f2 f3 f4 coolpix frankartculinaryyahoode italien italia venedig venezia venise murano burano square places place plaza plätze strasen rue calle strada streets historic market gondolas gondoliere yacht sailing piazza sanmarco gondeln gondole góndola voga canalgrande rialto cruiseliner
description: Italy, Venice, …watch your head,…oops, he was lucky ,…I guess, if a gondolier does not know the bridges in Venice,  then he has the wrong job.  There are officially 425 Gondolas, 2010, in Venice with a license, which is hard to get & stays normally in the family. A license for a Gondoliere can cost up to 500.000 €, on the other hand, a Gondoliere makes between 10 & 15 thousand € a month, …stand 2010  Modern gondolas are up to 11 mtr long, 1.6 mtr wide & a weight of about 350 kg, made of 280 hand-made pieces using eight types of wood. It takes about 200 hours to manufacture a gondola; at a cost of roughly 30,000 €.  To compensate for unilateral propulsion, the gondola body is built asymmetrically along the central axis; the left side is curved more than the right starboard side, so that its contour on the starboard side is about 0.25 m shorter than on the port side.  The several mtr long oar-paddle is held in an o complicated shaped fork, known as a Fórcola, which is inserted into a rectangular opening in the hull on the starboard side.  The Forcola is allowing several positions of the oar for slow forward rowing, powerful forward rowing, turning, slowing down, rowing backwards stopping, shaped individually for each gondoliers style.  The ornament iron, called the "fero da prorà" or "dolfin", on the front of the boat is made from brass, stainless steel, or aluminium. It serves as decoration; every detail of the gondola has its own symbolism & needed to balance the weight of the gondolier at the stern, has an "S" shape symbolic of the twists in the Canal Grande. Under the main blade, there is a kind of comb with six teeth or prongs pointing forward standing for the six districts of Venice. A kind of tooth juts out backwards toward the centre of the gondola symbolises the island of Giudecca, the curved top signifies the Doge's cap.  Originally, the designs were not uniform & changed over the centuries. Originally, there were gondolas in all possible colours. The Venetian noble & patrician houses at the time tried to outdo each other in the magnificent equipment of the boats. In order to put an end to this unbridled pomposity, the Senate of Venice in 1562 passed the expense law. Supported by the church, this law prescribed uniform black equipment for all gondolas; there were exceptions for foreign envoys & for festivals.  👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  16 million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: Italy, Venice, street musicians playing by the towers of “Arsenale” for the restaurant outdoor customers at Campo de Arsenale The entrance to the “Arsenale” at Campo de Arsenale with the wooden bridge crossing the Rio dell' Arsenale The Arsenale used to be originally Venice’s shipyard, founded in 1104. Nowadays it belongs to the Italian marine corps & not accessible to the public, the only exception is every two years during the “Biennale”, the oldest established forum for contemporary art.  The arsenal was surrounded by walls & towers like a fortress, to which there were only two entrances; the water gate, "Ingresso all'Acqua", which is flanked by two towers erected in 1574 & if necessary, could be closed off by a gate. Through this gate, shipping traffic was handled over the harbour basin into the Mediterranean.  The land gate, portal gate - "Ingresso di Terra". The Land Gate is located next to the Water Gate, which in its current form was built around 1460 in the manner of a triumphal arch to show that Venice is famous for its wealth & fortune. The entrance stairs are flanked by 8 symbolic figures. The two giant ancient lions to the right & left of the stairs are loot pieces that Francesco Morosini brought from Greece to Venice in 1687. The left three-meter-high lion had been near Athens in Porto Leone for two thousand years.  The lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. If the buildings would be straight, painted & well maintained, I think Venice without this patina, the gondolas & gondolieri, channels, little bridges & the at all-time busy Canal Grande; all these main ingredients altogether express the romantic charm of Venice.    👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  14  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

tags: italy venice nikon d800 d300 d200 f2 f3 f4 coolpix frankartculinaryyahoode square places place plaza plätze strasen rue calle strada streets historic market italien italia venedig venezia venise murano burano gondolas gondoliere yacht sailing piazza sanmarco gondeln gondole góndola voga whl394
description: Italy, Venice,…a rare view on the Piazzetta of the San Marco’s square on early Friday morning, …the tourists still in the hotels having their breakfast ...the pigeons are already approaching San Marco square &  ...the arriving cruise liners still haven’t spit out the thousands of the 6 hours daytime tourist yet.   The extension through the so-called Piazzetta, it extends to the Doge's Palace & opens there to the lagoon, the Bacino di San Marco at the beginning of the Grand Canal.  From here you can start a trip with a Gondola, taking a water-taxi or the "Vaporetti". The vaporetto is the only real public transport in Venice. It is a passenger ship, which is used in regular transport like a bus. There are about 20 lines in total. The ferries are used on the one hand within the island of Venice & also connect the main island with other islands like Murano, Burano or the cemetery island, they also connect Venice with the airport, the train station & the mainland.  In front of the San Marcos Cathedral are three ship masts since 1480, on which the Markus banner flies on Sundays & public holidays.  👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  16  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: Italy, Venice, "Mercato del Pesce al Minuto" near Ponte Rialto. Seafood in great variety & of the highest quality of the particular menagerie of scaled, tentacled & hard-shelled sea creatures are on display at the market on five mornings a week.  Freshest & finest quality is an obsession by the only less than 70,000 inhabitants of Venice. The market is not huge, about thirty fishmongers & their assistants stand behind tables glistening with fish guts & brine beneath a canvas canopy supported by colonnades in the Venetian neo-Gothic style. The present construction dates only from the early 20th century, but seafood has been sold at the Rialto for at least 500 years. An inscription in ornamental metalwork over a nearby gate reads "Piscis premium a capite Foetet"- Fish starts rotting from the head", this old saying once had political as well as piscatorial character.  The assortment of seafood differs from season to season & even can varies from day to day. Unless the fish is identified as frozen, there is a bit of this, obviously dried or salted, you may be sure that everything sold here is as fresh as it can be.  Not everything in the market is fished in local waters, the fishing grounds of the northern Adriatic are closed annually for 40 days between July & September to allow stocks to regenerate, during that period everything comes from somewhere else, except for the fish, including the famous & delicate branzini, or sea bass, that are farmed in the area, but what does come from the lagoon usually sells for a premium a local seafood & is labelled "Nostrano", "ours," a standard term all over Italy.  The choice & freshness of the fish & seafood displayed on ice is hard to beat; comparable in central Europe I have only found in France & the Mediterranean region, even so we have a good fish market in Hamburg, but cannot be compared to this choice.  The lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. If the buildings would be straight, painted & well maintained, I think Venice without this patina, the gondolas & gondolieri, channels, little bridges & the at all-time busy Canal Grande; all these main ingredients altogether express the romantic charm of Venice.    👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  13  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

title: VENEZIA. PONTE DEI FRARI.
tags: 1254 77 38 venezia ve venice venedig venecia serenissima ponte calle rio canale pontedeifrari turisti gente people tourist veneto palazzi house campodeifrari italia italy italien italie bellitalia architettura nordest canon eos6dmarkii 6dmarkii canoneos6dmarkii canon6dmarkii franco600d jewel
description: VERSO LA BASILICA.    Il ponte dei Frari fu fatto erigere la prima volta dai padri francescani nel 1428. I frati consideravano sacro il ponte, e dunque luogo d’immunità per i malviventi. Questo perché, prima che venisse costruita la chiesa così come appare oggi, quella precedente era orientata esattamente in senso opposto. La Basilica di Santa Maria Gloriosa dei Frari , per tutti nota come chiesa dei Frari, è una delle maggiori e più antiche chiese di Venezia. È situata nel campo dei Frari, nel cuore del sestiere di San Polo, e al suo interno sono custodite numerose opere d’arte. La storia di questo luogo inizia con la concessione ai francescani di un pezzo di terra per costruire una piccola chiesa, dedicata a Santa Maria. Tuttavia la costruzione fu ampliata e completata nel 1338. Cotto e pietra d’Istria definiscono i colori di questa monumentale chiesa gotica che custodisce all’interno capolavori assoluti di Tiziano: la Pala Pesaro e l’Assunta. Nella chiesa si trova anche la tomba del pittore cadorino.    CANON EOS 6D Mark II con ob. CANON EF 24-85 f./3,5-4,5 USM

description: Italy, Venice, Piazza San Marco, the “Caffè Florian” under the arcades of the “Procuratie Nuove” at the San Marco Square was founded by the Cafétier Floriano Francesconi in December 1720 under the name "Venezia Trionfante", Triumphant Venice, but the Venetian only called the Café by the first name of the owner & so it was changed later into “Caffè Florian”.   The Café used to be the place to meet for Italy’s patriots, artists & intellectuals as well as international well-known personalities; today’s patrons are basically tourists.  The lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. If the venetian buildings would be straight, painted & well maintained, without their unique buildings & their patina, the gondolas, channels, little bridges & the at all-time busy Canale Grande; these main ingredients altogether express the romantic charm of Venice,…I think.   👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  13  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: Italy, Venice, Piazza San Marco, …no matter the kind of weather,  ...10 am, ready for cappuccino-business with live music at the "Caffé Lavena" on Piazza San Marco, established in 1750.   In 1860, Carlo Lavena bought the café & it has remained in his family ever since. The Lavena had its part of intellectual life in the city, the person who gave shine to Caffé Lavena, patronizing it from his first coming to Venice & becoming a habitual customer, was the composer Richard Wagner. Almost every day between five to six in the afternoon,   Wagner used to visit Caffé Lavena & remained for half an hour, chitchatting very often with the owner Carlo Lavena. However, there were many other popular societies. Today along with the current plethora of celebrated & not so famous movie stars that visits Venice during the annual Biennale Film Festival, the café is frequented by gondoliers, Venetians & tourists alike.  👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  16 million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: Italy, Venice, "Campo Bandiera & Moro o de la Bragora" with a peaceful atmosphere, the square is framed by the church of San Giovanni in Bragora & some beautiful buildings including the Palazzo Gritti-Badoer.  Siting on the benches for a break in the shade of the trees at the square, you can observe the "original fake merchandise" street vendors who are meeting or passing by here. However, while I was sitting here, I did not see once a vendor approaching or harassing anyone on the marketplace.  The origin of the term Bragora is unclear, but relates to the church "San Giovanni in Bragora", located on the square & founded in the early 8th century, Pietro Barbo, 1417-1471, future Pope Paul II & Antonio Vivaldi, 1678 -1741, were baptised in the church.   👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  16  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

title: Phot.Venice.Gondola.01.041025.5222.jpg
tags: italy venice nikon d800 d300 d200 f2 f3 f4 coolpix frankartculinaryyahoode italien italia venedig venezia venise murano burano square places place plaza plätze strasen rue calle strada streets historic market gondolas gondoliere yacht sailing piazza sanmarco gondeln gondole góndola voga canalgrande rialto
description: Italy, Venice, Canal Grande Modern gondolas are up to 11 mtr long, 1.6 mtr wide & a weight of about 350 kg, made of 280 hand-made pieces using eight types of wood. It takes about 200 hours to manufacture a gondola; at a cost of roughly 30,000 €.  The oar-paddle, which is several meters long, lies in a fork, forcola, which is inserted into a rectangular opening in the hull on the starboard side. To compensate for unilateral propulsion, the gondola body is built asymmetrically along the central axis; the left side is curved more than the right starboard side, so that its contour on the starboard side is about 0.25 m shorter than on the port side. The oar-paddle is held in an o complicated shaped fork, known as a Fórcola, allowing several positions of the oar for slow forward rowing, powerful forward rowing, turning, slowing down, rowing backwards stopping, shaped individually for each gondoliers style. The ornament iron, called the "fero da prorà" or "dolfin", on the front of the boat is made from brass, stainless steel, or aluminium. It serves as decoration; every detail of the gondola has its own symbolism & needed to balance the weight of the gondolier at the stern, has an "S" shape symbolic of the twists in the Canal Grande. Under the main blade, there is a kind of comb with six teeth or prongs pointing forward standing for the six districts of Venice. A kind of tooth juts out backwards toward the centre of the gondola symbolises the island of Giudecca, the curved top signifies the Doge's cap.  Originally, the designs were not uniform & changed over the centuries. Originally, there were gondolas in all possible colours. The Venetian noble & patrician houses at the time tried to outdo each other in the magnificent equipment of the boats. In order to put an end to this unbridled pomposity, the Senate of Venice in 1562 passed the expense law. Supported by the church, this law prescribed uniform black equipment for all gondolas; there were exceptions for foreign envoys & for festivals.  👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  13  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: Italy, Venice, ...arriving at six thirty am at daybreak in Venice.  Due to the volcanic eruption of the "Eyjafjallajökull" in Iceland, all flights were cancelled & I had to rebook going by train.  Departure in Hamburg on Friday at 1 pm, in Munich train change with a 2 hour stay, in Verona at 3 am 1 hour stop at the train station staying in the train, arrival in Venice on Saturday at 6 am, arrival at the hotel, 2 minutes from Marcus square, at 7 am, I checked in but rooms are only ready for occupancy by new arrivals at 1 pm, what is usually the normal check-in time in hotels,  …all in all a 24 hour trip, …one experience richer &  regrets or frustration, …none.  If I had flown on Saturday as planed & booked, I would have arrived at 1 pm & probably would have never seen Venice from the sea side at daybreak,...cosi, tutto bene.  👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  16  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

title: VENEZIA. FONDAMENTA DEI FRARI.
tags: venezia venecia venice venedig serenissima veneto calle ponte bridge rio rioterrà barche street fondamentadeifrari pizzeria puente pont brücke palazzi palace building palais italia italy italie italien bellitalia canon eos6dmarkii 6dmarkii canoneos6dmarkii canon6dmarkii franco600d 1001nightsthenew 1001nightsthenewmagiccity 1001nightsmagicwindow 1001nightsmagichall 2911 52 62
description: TRA CALLI, PONTI E RII TERRA'.   A Venezia ogni cosa è diversa dal resto del mondo ed ha un nome particolare che la caratterizza. Nemmeno le strade riescono ad essere semplici vie per trasportare persone e cose ma sono occasioni per vedere ad ogni passo dei luoghi magnifici.  Se a Venezia cercherete una strada, troverete solo Strada Nova, situata nel sestiere di Cannaregio, vera e propria arteria cittadina. Le altre vie a Venezia sono chiamate “Calli”, dal latino callis, che significa sentiero. Le calli possono essere molto strette oppure larghe, le “Calli Larghe”, possono essere cieche e chiamarsi “Rami”, quando portano ad un campo senza sbocco o direttamente ad un abitazione. A volte la necessità di creare spazi per le strade portava all’interramento di canali, facendoli diventare “Rio Terà” (canale interrato), dove spesso scorre tuttora l’acqua del canale sotto la pavimentazione stradale. description: IL CALDO E' OPPRIMENTE.   A Venezia, calli, calli larghe o, al diminutivo, callette e calleselle sono le vie, le strade che si intersecano per la città formando quel complicato dedalo nel quale è facile perdersi. Il termine Calle deriva dal latino callis, cha significa viottolo, sentiero, mulattiera. La larghezza delle calli può variare dai 0.60 mt. delle più strette ai 6 / 8 metri delle più larghe, che proprio per questo si chiamano "calle larga"(Calle Larga San Marco, Calle Larga XXII marzo). Anticamente tutto il territorio della città era di proprietà privata, ma quando venivano concessi dei permessi per costruire nuove costruzioni, la Repubblica obbligava i proprietari di lasciare un passaggio, che diventava pubblico, tra una proprietà e l'altra. title: VENEZIA. PONTE DEI FRARI.
tags: 1254 77 38 venezia ve venice venedig venecia serenissima ponte calle rio canale pontedeifrari turisti gente people tourist veneto palazzi house campodeifrari italia italy italien italie bellitalia architettura nordest canon eos6dmarkii 6dmarkii canoneos6dmarkii canon6dmarkii franco600d jewel
description: VERSO LA BASILICA.    Il ponte dei Frari fu fatto erigere la prima volta dai padri francescani nel 1428. I frati consideravano sacro il ponte, e dunque luogo d’immunità per i malviventi. Questo perché, prima che venisse costruita la chiesa così come appare oggi, quella precedente era orientata esattamente in senso opposto. La Basilica di Santa Maria Gloriosa dei Frari , per tutti nota come chiesa dei Frari, è una delle maggiori e più antiche chiese di Venezia. È situata nel campo dei Frari, nel cuore del sestiere di San Polo, e al suo interno sono custodite numerose opere d’arte. La storia di questo luogo inizia con la concessione ai francescani di un pezzo di terra per costruire una piccola chiesa, dedicata a Santa Maria. Tuttavia la costruzione fu ampliata e completata nel 1338. Cotto e pietra d’Istria definiscono i colori di questa monumentale chiesa gotica che custodisce all’interno capolavori assoluti di Tiziano: la Pala Pesaro e l’Assunta. Nella chiesa si trova anche la tomba del pittore cadorino.    CANON EOS 6D Mark II con ob. CANON EF 24-85 f./3,5-4,5 USM

title: Venezia, Rio Marin.
tags: venezia veneto italy italia europa europe flickr foto canali calle olympussp590uz hdr laguna canale
description: Indossa occhiali da sole molto scuri: proteggiti. Venezia può essere letale. In centro storico la radioattività estetica è altissima. Ogni scorcio irradia bellezza; apparentemente dismessa: profondamente subdola, inesorabile. Il sublime gronda a secchiate dalle chiese, ma anche le calli senza monumenti, i ponticelli sui rii sono come minimo pittoreschi. Le facciate dei palazzi sono colpi di faccia, come le pedate sono colpi di piede. ….. Ti senti male. E’ il famoso disturbo di monsieur Henri Beyle, malore passato alla storia come sindrome di Stendhal. ….. Se ti basta una passeggiata di qualche ora a ridurti così, pensa che cosa dovrebbero dire i veneziani. Tiziano Scarpa

title: View from Fondamenta de la Fenice, San Marco, Venezia, Italy
tags: ponte fondamenta fenice sanmarco venezia veneto italia canal bridge window reflection balcony brick rendering rio veste menuo verona venice italy calle weathering
description: The bridge beneath the street light carries Calle Fenice over the junction of Rio de la Veste (in the foreground) and Rio Menuo o de la Verona (joining from the left). The building on the right is the internationally renowned Teatro la Fenice, 'The Phoenix', opened in 1792 to replace one lost to fire in 1774 (catastrophic fire seems to be an international tradition amongst opera houses). This then, er, burned down in 1836... and again in 1996. In the intervening periods, this has been the location of many famous operatic premieres. The warm light cast on the left wall is from the canopied canalside entrance, rather more prestigious than the landward 'main' entrance on Campo San Fantin.

title: VENEZIA. LA VIA DI FUGA.
tags: venezia venedig venice venecia veneto italia italy italie italien bellitalia canale rio calle passeggiata gondola gondoliere turisti turismo serenissima centrostorico centrocittà unesco patrimoniodellumanità fascino storia canon eos600d sigma franco600d 2716 62 46
description: C'E' CHI VA A PIEDI E CHI IN GONDOLA....   Prima che la gondola diventasse di esclusivo uso turistico, e cioè da pochi decenni, al centro della stessa veniva montata una copertura invernale e notturna chiamata felze, che era dotata di una porta e di finestre scorrevoli con veneziane e tendine, di specchi e di uno scaldino; serviva a proteggere i passeggeri sia dal freddo sia dagli sguardi indiscreti; oggi è quasi completamente in disuso in quanto ostacola la visibilità e, quindi, è poco indicata per il turista. La costruzione di una gondola può richiedere parecchi mesi e comporta circa 500 ore lavorative. Considerando che una gondola dura mediamente 20 anni, si può stimare che a Venezia servono circa 20 gondole l’anno.   CANON EOS 600D con ob. SIGMA 10-20 f./4-5,6 EX DC HSM

title: VENEZIA. ANTICHI MATTONI.
tags: venezia venedig venice serenissima canale rio veneto case mattoni intonaco finestre cannaregio calle bellitalia italia italy ghetto acqua canon eos600d sigma franco600d 5014 105 84
description: UN CANALE A CANNAREGIO.  A Canneregio,dal Campo di Sant’Alvise, tagliando in diagonale in direzione del centro del quartiere, oltrepassando la calle di Capitelli, la calle della Malvasia e le Fondamenta degli Ormesini si giunge al quartiere ebraico, il quartiere dove fino dal 1516 per volere della Signoria dovevano abitare gli ebrei residenti a Venezia. La Serenissima, pur difendendo l’integrità della fede cattolica, cercava di mantenere una certa tolleranza verso tutte le fedi religiose a patto che non facessero opera di proselitismo o non dessero scandalo. Tale politica era dettata dall’esigenza di mantenere buoni rapporti politici e commerciali con culture e religioni diverse. Gli ebrei rappresentavano a Venezia il tramite per il commercio con il mondo greco. Tuttavia la loro presenza suscitava malumori da parte dei cristiani anche per l’invidia che gli ebrei scatenava a causa delle loro ricchezze, molti di loro erano infatti banchieri o usurai ma ciò si spiega con il fatto che era proibito loro esercitare le arti manuali. Si decise, nel 1516 di relegarli in una piccola insula circondata da un anello d’acqua, posta a Cannaregio, vicino a San Geremia chiamata Ghetto Vecchio. L’origine di questo nome pare provenga dal fatto che in questa zona un tempo esistevano delle fonderie pubbliche dove si gettavano, cioè si fondavano le bombarde e, per traslazione, questo termine divenne sinonimo di ambiente coattivamente abitato da ebrei. title: VENEZIA. ANTICHI MATTONI.
tags: venezia venedig venice serenissima canale rio veneto case mattoni intonaco finestre cannaregio calle bellitalia italia italy ghetto acqua canon eos600d sigma franco600d 5014 105 84
description: UN CANALE A CANNAREGIO.  A Canneregio,dal Campo di Sant’Alvise, tagliando in diagonale in direzione del centro del quartiere, oltrepassando la calle di Capitelli, la calle della Malvasia e le Fondamenta degli Ormesini si giunge al quartiere ebraico, il quartiere dove fino dal 1516 per volere della Signoria dovevano abitare gli ebrei residenti a Venezia. La Serenissima, pur difendendo l’integrità della fede cattolica, cercava di mantenere una certa tolleranza verso tutte le fedi religiose a patto che non facessero opera di proselitismo o non dessero scandalo. Tale politica era dettata dall’esigenza di mantenere buoni rapporti politici e commerciali con culture e religioni diverse. Gli ebrei rappresentavano a Venezia il tramite per il commercio con il mondo greco. Tuttavia la loro presenza suscitava malumori da parte dei cristiani anche per l’invidia che gli ebrei scatenava a causa delle loro ricchezze, molti di loro erano infatti banchieri o usurai ma ciò si spiega con il fatto che era proibito loro esercitare le arti manuali. Si decise, nel 1516 di relegarli in una piccola insula circondata da un anello d’acqua, posta a Cannaregio, vicino a San Geremia chiamata Ghetto Vecchio. L’origine di questo nome pare provenga dal fatto che in questa zona un tempo esistevano delle fonderie pubbliche dove si gettavano, cioè si fondavano le bombarde e, per traslazione, questo termine divenne sinonimo di ambiente coattivamente abitato da ebrei.  CANON EOS 600D con ob. SIGMA 10-20 f./4-5,6 EX DC HSM

description: Italy, Venice, Santa Maria della Salute, the Roman Catholic Church is commonly known simply as “La Salute” on the Grand Canal, to be found at Punta della Dogana in the Dorsoduro. The Dom is one of the largest churches of Venice & has the status of a minor basilica. It stands at the junction between the Canale Grande & the “Bacino di San Marco” on the lagoon. In October 1630, the Venetian Senate decreed that if the city was delivered from the currently raging plague that had killed about a third of Venice's population, then a new church would be built and dedicated to the Virgin Mary.  The city was so delivered & Baldassare Longhena, then only 26 years old, was selected to design the new church. It was consecrated in 1681, the year before Longhena's death & completed in 1687.  👉 One World one Dream, ...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  7,5 million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

Gli ebrei rappresentavano a Venezia il tramite per il commercio con il mondo greco. Tuttavia la loro presenza suscitava malumori da parte dei cristiani anche per l’invidia che gli ebrei scatenava a causa delle loro ricchezze, molti di loro erano infatti banchieri o usurai ma ciò si spiega con il fatto che era proibito loro esercitare le arti manuali. Si decise, nel 1516 di relegarli in una piccola insula circondata da un anello d’acqua, posta a Cannaregio, vicino a San Geremia chiamata Ghetto Vecchio. L’origine di questo nome pare provenga dal fatto che in questa zona un tempo esistevano delle fonderie pubbliche dove si gettavano, cioè si fondavano le bombarde e, per traslazione, questo termine divenne sinonimo di ambiente coattivamente abitato da ebrei. title: Libreria Acqua Alta in Venice
tags: old venice water colors eos calle library bookstore used alta acqua venezia colori channel libreria 24105 usato 5dmarkii eos5dmarkii canonef24105mm140l
description: A stop at Libreria Acqua Alta in Venice is a must for book lovers One of the most original libraries in the world is located in Venice. Libreria Acqua Alta, in Calle Lunga Santa Maria Formosa, is definitely the only place on earth where you will find a huge selection of new and used books set out in undoubtedly special shelving: boats, gondolas, canoes, tanks and anywhere that it is possible to insert them. And the books are not only for buying but have been turned into true objects of furniture. Old encyclopaedias, the kind that no one buys any more, become steps in an amazing staircase, or cover the walls of the outer courtyards, transforming them into colourful surfaces. To complete the furnishings, there are also poles, oars and mannequins.  Per gli amanti dei libri è d’obbligo una tappa alla Libreria Acqua Alta a Venezia Una delle librerie più originali al mondo si trova a Venezia. La Libreria Acqua Alta, in Calle Lunga Santa Maria Formosa, è sicuramente l’unico posto sulla terra dove troverete una vastissima scelta di libri, nuovi e usati,  disposti all’interno di scaffali decisamente particolari: barche, gondole, canoe, vasche e dovunque sia possibile infilarli. E i libri non solo soltanto da comprare ma sono stati trasformati in veri e propri oggetti d’arredamento. Le vecchie enciclopedie, quelle che ormai non compra più nessuno, possono diventare gradini per una strepitosa scalinata, o ricoprire le pareti delle corti esterne trasformandole in superfici coloratissime. A completare l’arredamento ci pensano anche paline, remi, manichini.

title: el sotopòrtego
tags: venice italy calle italia corte venezia cannaregio sotoportego urbanistica fondamentanuove sottoporticato canon70d antomarto ntomarto corteberendis berendis
description: Il "sotopòrtego" (letteralmente, "sotto porticato") è uno degli elementi più caratteristici dell'urbanistica della città di Venezia. Si tratta infatti di un passaggio di collegamento tra calli o altre strade, ricavato direttamente nel corpo di un edificio, tipicamente eliminandone una porzione di altezza pari a tutto il piano terreno.  Molto spesso il sotopòrtego costituisce l'unico accesso a una corte o a un campiello. ________________________________________________  "Sotopòrtego" (under arcade) is one of the most characteristic elements of urbanism in the city of Venice. It is a connecting passage between "calli" (calli , in Venice, are roads between very close buildings) or other roads, formed directly in the body of a building, typically eliminating a portion of a height equal to the entire ground floor.  Very often "sotoportego" is the only access to a court or a "campiello" (little square).

description: Italy, Venice, the Canal Grande at twilight. The last "S" shaped, almost 4 km of the river Brenta is Venice's main waterway, the Canal Grande, over 40 smaller canals flow into the Grand Canal.  There are currently four bridges spanning the canal, the oldest of which is the Rialto Bridge, a covered limestone bridge built at the end of the 16th century instead of a wooden bridge. Until the Scalzi Bridge & Accademia Bridge, built in the 19th century, the Rialto Bridge was the only permanent link across the canal. title: el sotopòrtego
tags: venice italy calle italia corte venezia cannaregio sotoportego urbanistica fondamentanuove sottoporticato canon70d antomarto ntomarto corteberendis berendis
description: Il "sotopòrtego" (letteralmente, "sotto porticato") è uno degli elementi più caratteristici dell'urbanistica della città di Venezia. Si tratta infatti di un passaggio di collegamento tra calli o altre strade, ricavato direttamente nel corpo di un edificio, tipicamente eliminandone una porzione di altezza pari a tutto il piano terreno.  Molto spesso il sotopòrtego costituisce l'unico accesso a una corte o a un campiello. ________________________________________________  "Sotopòrtego" (under arcade) is one of the most characteristic elements of urbanism in the city of Venice. It is a connecting passage between "calli" (calli , in Venice, are roads between very close buildings) or other roads, formed directly in the body of a building, typically eliminating a portion of a height equal to the entire ground floor.  Very often "sotoportego" is the only access to a court or a "campiello" (little square).

title: Naomi (Stranger #19/100), Venezia San Marco
tags: street venice portrait eye sunglasses project calle arch strangers alleyway 100 contact venezia softbox reflector degli albanese
description: The Venice trip marks a milestone in my (early) 100 Stranger's Project; the point where my approach has (perhaps temporarily) crystalised to (a) find a background (b) determine exposure (c) wait for the right stranger (d) shoot. In some cases that gobbled up a little too much time, waiting for the right stranger to come by. In this case, Naomi turned up and kindly agreed to join in. She was over from England on a photography trip with 100 others, and had just taken a solo trip out from the rest of the group. This is just inside one of the side alleys running parallel to the "Bridge of Sighs". Naomi is holding a gold striped reflector and I've shooting with an on camera softbox, to get a decent exposure in the shadows. It was a little tricky to judge - thank you Naomi for being patient on that front. It felt important to make sure the distant light of the alleyway was darker than her face, to make sure the emphasis was correctly balanced to give her centre stage. Thanks Naomi for taking part in the project - as per our chat, I can't recommend it enough and really hope you decide to start one of your own. In any case, hopefully you got some great shots of Venice on your trip. This is portrait #19 of my 100 Strangers Project - check out the group page and get involved.

description: Italy, Venice, Piazza San Marco, a bad day for Cappuccino tourist business at "Caffé Lavena" on Piazza San Marco, established in 1750.   In 1860, Carlo Lavena bought the café & it has remained in his family ever since. The Lavena had its part of intellectual life in the city, the person who gave shine to Caffé Lavena, patronizing it from his first coming to Venice & becoming a habitual customer, was the composer Richard Wagner. Almost every day between five to six in the afternoon,   Wagner used to visit Caffé Lavena & remained for half an hour, chitchatting very often with the owner Carlo Lavena. However, there were many other popular societies. Today along with the current plethora of celebrated & not so famous movie stars that visits Venice during the annual Biennale Film Festival, the café is frequented by gondoliers, Venetians & tourists alike.  👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  13  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: Italy, Venice, “Piazzetta di San Marco” with its two columns facing the gondolas on the lagoon. The “Lion of Venice” with its approximate weight of 3 tons surmounts one of two large granite columns in the Square, thought to have been erected about 1268 carrying ancient symbols of the two patron saints of Venice. The eastern column bears a creature which at some point came to represent the “Lion of Saint Mark”, traditional symbol of the evangelist. The sculpture has had a very long & obscure history, probably starting its existence as a winged lion-griffin statue on a monument to the god “Sandon” at Tarsus in Cilicia, Southern Turkey about 300 BC.  Executions of declared death sentences were carried out between the freestanding columns in the piazzetta next to the lagoon. Even today Venetians won't walk between the two columns; a final chance to live was given if you could walk around the edge of the third column on the seaward side of the palace, …no one ever succeeded.  👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  16 million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: Italy, Venice,  …it's raining, it's pouring, a gondliere life can be boring…,  Gondolas parked at Fondamento Orseo, next to San Marcos square. There are officially 425 Gondolas in Venice with a license, which is hard to get & stays normally in the Family. A license for a Gondoliere can cost up to 500.000 €, on the other hand, a Gondoliere makes between 10 & 15 thousand € a month, …stand 2010  Modern gondolas are up to 11 mtr long, 1.6 mtr wide & a weight of about 350 kg, made of 280 hand-made pieces using eight types of wood. It takes about 200 hours to manufacture a gondola; at a cost of roughly 30,000 €.  The oar-paddle, which is several meters long, lies in a fork, forcola, which is inserted into a rectangular opening in the hull on the starboard side. To compensate for unilateral propulsion, the gondola body is built asymmetrically along the central axis; the left side is curved more than the right starboard side, so that its contour on the starboard side is about 0.25 m shorter than on the port side. The oar-paddle is held in an o complicated shaped fork, known as a Fórcola, allowing several positions of the oar for slow forward rowing, powerful forward rowing, turning, slowing down, rowing backwards stopping, shaped individually for each gondoliers style. The ornament iron, called the "fero da prorà" or "dolfin", on the front of the boat is made from brass, stainless steel, or aluminium. It serves as decoration; every detail of the gondola has its own symbolism & needed to balance the weight of the gondolier at the stern, has an "S" shape symbolic of the twists in the Canal Grande. Under the main blade, there is a kind of comb with six teeth or prongs pointing forward standing for the six districts of Venice. A kind of tooth juts out backwards toward the centre of the gondola symbolises the island of Giudecca, the curved top signifies the Doge's cap.  Originally, the designs were not uniform & changed over the centuries. Originally, there were gondolas in all possible colours. The Venetian noble & patrician houses at the time tried to outdo each other in the magnificent equipment of the boats. In order to put an end to this unbridled pomposity, the Senate of Venice in 1562 passed the expense law. Supported by the church, this law prescribed uniform black equipment for all gondolas; there were exceptions for foreign envoys & for festivals.  👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  13  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments
