description: Italy, Venice, oarswomen training for the “Vogalonga”, the long row is a game & a protest. The 32 kilometres rowing regatta starts at St. Mark’s Basin, moves towards the islands of Vignole, Sant´Erasmo & Burano, the northern lagoon through Murano, the Rio Cannaregio, on to the new Venetia, then continues past the Palazzi of the Grand Canale & finishes at the Punta della Dogana, opposite St. Mark's Square. The famous Vogalonga regatta was founded in Venice in 1974, when a group of friends decided to hold a regatta, taking a stand against the increasing use of motorboats, the deterioration of the city & to celebrate Venetian traditions. The event takes place each year on a Sunday in May, when for a few hours the suffocating traffic of motorized boats ceases & the water is left alone to the rowers. The one day event, the 39th Vogalonga takes place in 2013 on Sunday, May 19. At the first official regatta, on May 8 1975, 500 boats from Venice & neighbouring cities took part, each year the regatta has grown in size & now over 1500 boats with about 6000 challengers participate each year in this colourful & spectacular sportive happening for all kinds, sizes & shapes of rowing boats.   ...Danke, Xièxie, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over 600.000 clicks in my photostream with countless motivating comments

title: Blend in with Venetians as they go about their daily lives
tags: world life voyage street old city trip travel venice houses windows light sea summer people italy music sun man color heritage water beauty weather river myself boats island mirror islands calle site al ancient alley topf50 colorful warm europe italia quiet ride ben taxi shoreline pedestrian tourist taxis canals unesco explore gondola moment topf100 venezia palaces italië venetië vaporetti 100faves 50faves brusa dolera atorno
description: © all rights reserved by B℮n Please take your time... to View it large on black Venice is a city in northeast Italy sited on a group of 118 small islands separated by canals and linked by bridges. It is located in the marshy Venetian Lagoon which stretches along the shoreline between the mouths of the Po and the Piave Rivers. Venice is renowned for the beauty of its setting, its architecture and its artworks.The city in its entirety is listed as World Heritage Site, along with its lagoon. The name is derived from the ancient Veneti people who inhabited the region by the 10th century B.C.Venice has decayed since its heyday and is heavily touristed ,there are slightly more tourists than residents, but the romantic charm remains. Venice, the world's only pedestrian city, is easily walkable, and the absence of cars makes this a particularly pleasant experience. However, walking and standing all day can also be exhausting, so it is best to pace yourself. The best way to get around a bit more quickly by numerous vaporetti water buses and water taxis. If you want to have a romantic ride along the canals, take a gondola ride. Venice is one of the busiest tourist destinations on the planet but If you wish to enjoy a quiet moment away from the popular tourist routes, just step in the authentically quiet city of Venice like here strolling along Calle Dolera Atorno Al Brusa. Lovely alleys and small squares feature the most authentic local atmospheres, where time stands still while you experience the true soul of Venice and blend in with Venetians as they go about their daily lives and traditions. Venetië is een stad in het noordoosten van Italië. Venetië is wereldwijd bekend om het historische centrum met zijn vele wateren en heeft een belangrijke rol gespeeld in de wereldgeschiedenis. Sinds 1987 staan Venetië en zijn lagune op de Werelderfgoedlijst van de UNESCO. Bijnamen van Venetië zijn onder meer stad van het water, stad van de bruggen. Venetië ligt op een groep kleine eilanden midden in een lagune die al in de tijd van het Romeinse Rijk Venetia werd genoemd. Er woonden al eeuwen mensen. Op 12 mei 1797 veroverde Napoleon de stad, waardoor Venetië na meer dan duizend jaar zijn onafhankelijkheid verloor. Auto's rijden er niet, de hoofdweg is een kanaal, het vervoermiddel is een boot. Lijnbussen, taxi's en ziekenwagens zijn in Venetië boten die zich al even ordeloos verplaatsen als de auto's in de grote Italiaanse steden. Venetië zelf bestaat uit 120 eilanden met elkaar verbonden door 150 kanalen en 400 bruggen.Het is de combinatie van zijn culturele en architecturale rijkdom met de unieke ligging die Venetië zo populair maakt bij de toeristen. Een wandeling door de vele kleine straatjes doet je écht kennismaken met de levenswijze van de Venetianen.

title: Hotel Cavalletto
tags: venice italy water reflections private gold hotel canal calle dock italia lagoon tourist 100views gondola idle venezia ripoff gondoliers 2600 cavalletto
description: Most of the canals in Venice, except for the Grand Canal are quite narrow, barely wide enough for 2 small boats to pass each other. So when we rounded the bend and found this scene with the gold color of the old hotel reflected in the gondola "parking lot", it was an "oohh, ahhh" moment ! I believe the number of gondola permits issued in Venice are quite restricted to prevent over-saturation of the market; nevertheless, most gondoliers seemed to be sitting around idle, trying to entice customers to pay the €90-€120 that they charge for a ride. I think if they lowered the price to €30-€40 per ride, they would be busy all day long.... but what do I know, I'm just a marketer (or racketeer as my husband frequently calls me.) cavalletto.hotelinvenice.com/overview.html Cavalletto and Doge Orseolo Hotel in Venice boasts a very long and highly considered experience. For more than seven centuries, in fact, it has been serving many important people, such as the Duke of Aosta, Richard Strauss and Winston Churchill. Because of its location and its services since 1308, Cavalletto and Doge Orseolo is considered one of the most ancient and famous hotels in Venice. By the way, the directions on the hotel's website are an indication of why people get lost in Venice: "You can reach the hotel by water bus (vaporetto) or by taxi. If you choose the vaporetto you have to take number 1 or 82 (or N1 or N82 during the night). After 45 minutes you have to get off at San Marco-Vallaresso. From there, just walk 2 minutes to the Hotel." Just walk 2 minutes to the hotel - which direction ? go straight? turn right? turn left? you could walk 2 minutes and land in the Grand Canal !

description: Calle Querini Estrecha calle en forma de "L" Desde Santa Maria Formosa a Sestiere Castello. Paseando por Venecia sin prisa, alejados de las rutas turísticas y sus bellos canales, se encuentran no menos bellos callejones por los que perderse. Perderse en el más amplio sentido de la palabra, callejones serpenteantes que no sabes sí vas a salir por la derecha, por la izquieda o qué posiblemente desembocan en la misma plaza de la que has salido, después de cruzar pequeños canalettos y adentrarte en algún bello "cortile" (ensanchamiento del callejón a modo de patio de viviendas).

title: Phot.Venice.Gondoliera.041029.6244.jpg

description: Italy, Venice, Venice’s only female Gondoliera, Alexandra Hai from Germany, known as Alex & as the rebel of the 21 century in Venice. She gave the local gondoliers a hard time never giving up her goal to become a gondoliera.   A court order of the administrative court allows her to work as a “Private Gondoliera”, at the same time not accepting the mafia manners which rule the city.  The building in the back is the Campo Franceso Morosini.  The lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. If the buildings would be straight, painted & well maintained, I think Venice without this patina, the gondolas & gondolieri, channels, little bridges & the at all-time busy Canal Grande; all these main ingredients altogether express the romantic charm of Venice.   👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  16 million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

title: Phot.Venice.Campo.Bragora.Moro.01.041025.4761.jpg
tags: nikon d300 d200 coolpix city art square places place plaza plätze strasen rue calle strada streets historic mall bar market städte ciudad ville citta venice venedig venezia
description: Italy, Venice, "Campo Bandiera & Moro o de la Bragora" with a peaceful atmosphere, the square is framed by the church of San Giovanni in Bragora & some beautiful buildings including the Palazzo Gritti-Badoer.  Siting on the benches for a break in the shade of the trees in the square, you can observe the "original fake merchandise" street vendors who are meeting or passing by here. However, while I was sitting here, I did not see once a vendor approaching or harassing anyone on the marketplace.  The origin of the term Bragora is unclear, but relates to the church "San Giovanni in Bragora", located on the square & founded in the early 8th century, Pietro Barbo, 1417-1471, future Pope Paul II & Antonio Vivaldi, 1678 -1741 were baptised in the church.   👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  13  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

title: Phot.Venice.Basilica.La.Salute.041024.6645.jpg
tags: china plaza city travel venice india streets reflection berlin art bar night mall germany munich münchen square noche calle nikon asia strada place market nacht zurich hamburg chinese beijing ciudad places historic coolpix nightlife d200 rue venezia nuit venedig städte ville citta d300 strasen plätze colorphotoaward flickrstruereflection1
description: Italy, Venice, “Basilica di Santa Maria della Salute”, commonly known simply as “La Salute”, is one of the largest churches of Venice & has the status of a minor basilica. It stands at the junction between the Canale Grande & the “Bacino di San Marco” on the lagoon. In October 1630, the Venetian Senate decreed that if the city was delivered from the currently raging plague that had killed about a third of Venice's population, then a new church would be built and dedicated to the Virgin Mary.  The city was so delivered & Baldassare Longhena, then only 26 years old, was selected to design the new church. It was consecrated in 1681, the year before Longhena's death & completed in 1687.

description: Italy, Venice, Piazza San Marco, the “Caffè Florian” under the arcades of the “Procuratie Nuove” at the San Marco Square was founded by the Cafétier Floriano Francesconi in December 1720 under the name "Venezia Trionfante", Triumphant Venice, but the Venetian only called the Café by the first name of the owner & so it was changed later into “Caffè Florian”.   The Café used to be the place to meet for Italy’s patriots, artists & intellectuals as well as international well-known personalities; today’s patrons are basically tourists.  The lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. If the venetian buildings would be straight, painted & well maintained, without their unique buildings & their patina, the gondolas, channels, little bridges & the at all-time busy Canale Grande; these main ingredients altogether express the romantic charm of Venice,…I think.   👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  13  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: Italy, Venice, water taxi at the Puente Rialto.   ⦂⚪(‘..,Unfortunately I had not a good experience with a water taxi.  I booked my return trip to the airport with the water taxi via my longstanding, serious travel agency. The same price for the trip, € 95 for one or two person, so far so good. Leaving from the Piazza di San Marco, guests were then picked up from other hotels, which took a good 30 minutes until all 10 seats were taken & until the taxi driver took the trip to the airport, each customer paid the same price.  📌...a little tip,  1. if you take a water taxi, then go early & do not go the last minute if you dont want miss your flight back home,  2. with the water bus, the" Vaporetto", it works as well for just around € 7. Because the taxi, besides you, they collect more people until the taxi is full - total 10 pax - only than they hit the “road” to the airport.  I was the first in the taxi & pre-paid with luggade € 90 one way when i booked the trip, Until they had collected all the other people from the hotels, about 30 minutes had passed.  ......However, I had a great time in Venice.  👍...However, the lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. title: Phot.Venice.Harry's.Bar.Bellini.01.041025.5263.jpg
tags: nikon d300 d200 coolpix beijing china chinese city art nightlife night nacht noche nuit travel square places place plaza plätze strasen rue calle strada streets historic reflection mall bar market städte ciudad ville citta colorphotoaward india asia germany hamburg munich münchen berlin zurich venice venedig venezia d800
description: Italy, Venice, the first & original "Harry’s Bar", opened by Giuseppe Cipriani in Venice in March 1931, named after ”Harry Pickering” who gave Cipriani, a Bartender at the Hotel Europe, the first money to open his own bar, today there are numerous franchises worldwide,   Guiseppe Cipriano is also the creator, around in the forties, of the classic long drink “Bellini”, marrow from sun-ripened pink peaches & Prosecco Spumante the drink is simple but great & at 22€, 2010, a cocktail highly overpriced like all other cocktails at Harris Bar too.  By the way, the “Beef Carpaccio”, created at the five star hotel Cipriani & owner of the “Harry’s Bar”, still served in the original way, not thinly sliced on a meat slicing machine, rather the raw beef tenderloin is flattened between foil, seasoned with a creamy kind of mayonnaise dressing & served with a small side salad at over 60 € is also absolutely highly overpriced, even so it was good, ... in general food & beverage is tasty & well prepared,… however, prices are in no relation to quality & value,… well,... "Hemmingway's Ghost" makes  it possible.  📌... If it would not have been for Hemmingway, who choose this place as his favoured bar in Venice as his hangout & consequently also by many other famous customers from culture, art, show business/Hollywood & business “joint the club”. Just to mention a few like Charlie Chaplin, Princess Aspasia of Greece, Alfred Hitchcock, Jimmy Stewart, Truman Capote, Arturo Toscanini, Orson Welles, Baron Philippe de Rothschild, Aristotle Onassis, Barbara Hutton, Peggy Guggenheim, Woody Allen, George Cloone, & today mainly by simple tourists like me.  My main reason to visit the Harry’s Bar was simply because of my curiosity for food & beverage & gastronomical historically well-known locations. Without its past heritage it would be just another simple corner bar without any special flair & a service attitude like, "just another tourist-voyeur we will not see again", seems they don’t care about a good tip for adequate service.  Anyway, ... I was there, I saw & felt the "atmosphere", it was not good, it was not bad,....it was just the "opposite", ⦂⚪)… cheers…🍸,  ... like "Alfredos" restaurant in Rome, the creator of "Fettuccine Alfredo", I came, I saw & I'm enriched now for one more "one-time experience" & that's enough, "Veni, Vidi, Effugit",  👉 One World one Dream, ...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  16 million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments
